overview: Calvin Christian School offers the OJ Hofman Foundation Scholarship to assist students with the costs of transferring to the school from outside the Christian Schools Tasmania network. The scholarship recognises financial need and commitment to Christian education, helping families transition to Calvin Christian School.
description: The OJ Hofman Foundation Scholarship is designed to assist with the costs of transferring to Calvin Christian School. Through the OJ Hofman Scholarship Fund, named in honour of Calvin Christian School's founding Principal Oepke Jelte Hofman, this scholarship aims to make high-quality Christian Education affordable for all. The scholarship is available to students not currently enrolled at a Christian Schools Tasmania school. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to Christian Education, outline the costs of transitioning, and demonstrate financial need. The financial value varies based on individual circumstances and fund availability. Applications are reviewed by the Fund Committee and decisions are communicated in writing. Provision of the scholarship is dependent on ongoing enrolment at a Christian Schools Tasmania school and recipient must be a citizen or permanent resident of Australia.
eligibility_criteria: Students not currently enrolled at a Christian Schools Tasmania school; Commitment to Christian Education; Financial need demonstrated; Costs of transitioning outlined; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Secondary school students; Ongoing enrolment at CST school required
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School. To be eligible for a Christian Schools Tasmania scholarship, applicants must be citizens or permanent residents of Australia. Provision of a scholarship is dependent on ongoing enrolment at a Christian Schools Tasmania school. Annual review of scholarship status may occur.

overview: Calvin Christian School offers the OJ Hofman Extend Scholarship to support students in tutoring and development activities in areas of aptitude or excellence. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability and commitment to Christian education, providing financial support for skill development.
description: The OJ Hofman Extend Scholarship is designed to support students in tutoring and development activities in areas of aptitude or excellence. Through the OJ Hofman Scholarship Fund, this scholarship helps students pursue their talents and achieve excellence. The scholarship is available to current Christian Schools Tasmania students and prospective students, providing up to $3,000 per year for up to two years with annual review. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to Christian Education, provide evidence of their aptitude, and submit a proposed development plan along with evidence of opportunities for contributing to the school community. The scholarship supports development in areas where students show particular talent or excellence, enabling access to tutoring, coaching, or other developmental opportunities that might otherwise be financially inaccessible.
eligibility_criteria: Current Christian Schools Tasmania students or prospective students; Commitment to Christian Education; Evidence of aptitude and excellence in a specific area of educational achievement; Proposed development plan submitted; Opportunities for contributing to school community outlined; Australian citizen or permanent resident; Secondary school students; Ongoing enrolment at CST school required
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School. To be eligible for a Christian Schools Tasmania scholarship, applicants must be citizens or permanent residents of Australia. Provision of a scholarship is dependent on ongoing enrolment at a Christian Schools Tasmania school. Scholarship is awarded for up to two years with annual review. Recipients must maintain evidence of progress in their development plan and commitment to Christian Education.

overview: Calvin Christian School offers the OJ Hofman Foundation Scholarship to assist students with the costs of transferring to Calvin Christian School. The scholarship is designed to support students who demonstrate financial need and a commitment to Christian education.
description: The OJ Hofman Foundation Scholarship is available to students not currently enrolled at a Christian Schools Tasmania school who wish to transition to Calvin Christian School. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to Christian Education, outline the specific costs associated with their transition to the school, and demonstrate financial need. The scholarship value varies depending on individual circumstances and application review. The application process involves completing a detailed application form available on the school website, with review and recommendation by the School Principal and final approval by the CST OJ Hofman Scholarship Fund Committee. Scholarships are available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School. Provision of a scholarship is conditional upon ongoing enrolment at a Christian Schools Tasmania school. Scholarship recipients are reviewed annually. The special condition of ongoing annual review applies to this scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School; Students not currently enrolled at a Christian Schools Tasmania school; Must demonstrate commitment to Christian Education; Must outline costs of transitioning to Calvin; Must demonstrate financial need; Must be a citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Application form must include explanation of transitional costs and financial need demonstration
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School. Provision of a scholarship is conditional upon ongoing enrolment at a CST school. Scholarship recipients are reviewed annually. Applications must include detailed explanation of transitional costs and financial need.

overview: Calvin Christian School offers the OJ Hofman Extend Scholarship to support students in tutoring and development activities in areas of aptitude or excellence. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in specific areas of strength.
description: The OJ Hofman Extend Scholarship provides up to $3,000 per year to support secondary school students in tutoring and development activities that extend their abilities in areas of aptitude or excellence. These areas may include sport, music, arts, academic discipline, Christian ministry, or other fields where students demonstrate capacity for excellence. The scholarship is available to both current Christian Schools Tasmania students and prospective students. Applicants must demonstrate a commitment to Christian Education, provide evidence of their aptitude in their chosen area, and submit a proposed development plan detailing the tutoring, activities, or programs they will undertake to extend themselves. Recipients must also outline how they will contribute to the school community through their activities. The scholarship is awarded for up to two years, with an annual review process. Provision of a scholarship is conditional upon ongoing enrolment at a Christian Schools Tasmania school.
eligibility_criteria: Available to secondary school students at Calvin Christian School; Applicable to current Christian Schools Tasmania students and prospective students; Must demonstrate commitment to Christian Education; Must provide evidence of aptitude in chosen area of excellence; Must submit proposed development plan; Must be a citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Must contribute to school community; Application form completion required with evidence of aptitude and development plan
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for up to two years with an annual review. Provision of a scholarship is conditional upon ongoing enrolment at a CST school. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate evidence of their aptitude in their chosen area and submit a proposed development plan including details of tutoring, activities, or programs they will undertake to extend themselves in their chosen area. Recipients must contribute to the school community through their activities at or on behalf of the school.

overview: Calvin Christian School offers a generous Fee Assistance Program to support families who may experience difficulties meeting school fee obligations. The program is designed to make quality Christian education accessible to all families regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Calvin Christian School recognises that some families may face difficulties meeting school fee obligations. The school encourages parents and guardians who believe they will have financial challenges to enquire about the Fee Assistance Program. This program provides flexible support options to assist with tuition costs and is administered with sensitivity to family circumstances. The program is based on demonstrated financial need rather than merit-based criteria. Families can initiate a no obligation discussion with the school about what support may be available. Contact can be made directly with Christian Schools Tasmania accounts office or through the school's enrolments team to discuss individual circumstances and explore what assistance might be available. The program operates on a case-by-case basis with consideration of family financial situation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; No specific academic or merit requirements; Eligibility based on demonstrated financial need; Parents must contact school for no obligation discussion about fee assistance; Available to all year levels
special_conditions: Families experiencing difficulties meeting fee obligations should contact the school for a no obligation discussion. Fee Assistance Program is based on demonstrated financial need. Contact Mrs Joe-Ann Smit at Christian Schools Tasmania on (03) 6224 8300 or accounts@cst.tas.edu.au for enquiries.

overview: Channel Christian School offers a Fee Assistance Program to help families who have difficulty meeting school fee obligations. The school values Christian education and seeks to make it accessible to families in financial need.
description: Channel Christian School provides fee assistance to support families experiencing financial difficulties in meeting their tuition obligations. The school encourages parents who believe they will have difficulties meeting school fees to enquire about this generous program. The specific financial benefits, application process, and eligibility criteria are determined on a case-by-case basis through confidential discussion with the school. The program is administered to ensure that financial circumstances do not prevent access to Christian education at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Parents experiencing financial difficulty meeting school fee obligations; Families required to discuss circumstances directly with school
special_conditions: Families must contact the school directly for a no-obligation discussion about the program. All enquiries and discussions are treated confidentially.

overview: In honour of Calvin Christian School's Founding Principal, the OJ Hofman Scholarship Fund provides scholarships, bursaries and awards to promote educational opportunities for students at all Christian Schools Tasmania schools, including Channel Christian School.
description: The OJ Hofman Scholarship Fund is a charitable fund established to provide scholarships, bursaries and awards across Christian Schools Tasmania institutions. The fund supports educational opportunities for students, with specific allocation amounts and selection criteria determined by the Christian Schools Tasmania association. The fund is financed through tax-deductible donations from the community and charitable contributions. Families interested in applying or seeking more information should contact the school directly for details on current availability and selection processes.
eligibility_criteria: Students attending Christian Schools Tasmania schools including Channel Christian School; Specific eligibility determined by fund administrators
special_conditions: Administered by Christian Schools Tasmania association. Families must contact the school for current program details and application requirements.

school_name: Immaculate Heart of Mary Catholic School

overview: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) provides assistance to low income families towards the cost of levies for students enrolled at Launceston Big Picture School from Year 9 through to Year 12. The scheme is designed to support eligible families holding current Australian Government concession cards to help manage school costs.
description: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) provides assistance to low income families towards the cost of student levies. At Launceston Big Picture School, levies for 2024 are set at $550.00 per student. Eligible parents must hold a current concession card (Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Low Income Health Care Card, Services Australia Pensioner Concession Card, or Department of Veteran Affairs Pensioner Concession Card). Applications are made online via https://stasonlinesubmission.education.tas.gov.au/ and are processed within approximately 14 days. The scheme covers the full cost of levies for eligible families. Recipients who are already receiving student assistance do not need to complete another form when enrolling additional children. The school also offers flexible payment plans (weekly, fortnightly, or monthly) for families experiencing financial difficulty, and can direct families to external support through The Smith Family program for additional assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/guardian must hold a current Services Australia Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Low Income Health Care Card, Services Australia Pensioner Concession Card, or Department of Veteran Affairs Pensioner Concession Card; Student must be enrolled at Launceston Big Picture School in Year 9 through Year 12; Applicant must not already be receiving STAS for the same student
special_conditions: Applications are processed by the Department of Education within approximately 14 days of submission. If already receiving STAS, families do not need to reapply for additional enrolled children. The school offers flexible payment plan options (weekly, fortnightly, monthly) for families not eligible for STAS. Families ineligible for STAS may seek support through The Smith Family (phone: 6326 7898).

overview: Launceston Christian School offers the Association Scholarship to provide financial support to families towards school tuition fees. The scholarship recognises families who are members of or connected to the Launceston Christian School Association and assists with access to Christian education.
description: The Association Scholarship provides $2,000 per year for two years towards the family's tuition fees. This scholarship is designed to support families in accessing education at Launceston Christian School. The selection process involves an application submitted to the school, with consideration given to family circumstances and connection to the school community. The scholarship is awarded on an annual basis and families must maintain their Association status and comply with school conduct expectations during the tenure of the award.
eligibility_criteria: Family connection to Launceston Christian School Association; Current or prospective enrolment at the school; Demonstrated financial need; Good standing with school conduct and attendance policies
special_conditions: Scholarship is provided for a maximum of 2 years. Families must maintain good standing with school conduct and attendance expectations. Award is subject to annual review and renewal based on continued eligibility and school requirements.

overview: Launceston Christian School participates in the government Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) administered by the Department for Education, Children and Young People. The school provides tuition fee reductions to families approved under this scheme.
description: Families approved by the Department for Education, Children and Young People under the Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) receive a set reduction towards their tuition fees through the school's Finance Committee. Families who were approved and received STAS support in the previous year do not need to complete another application. New enrolments or families with changed circumstances are eligible to apply if a parent holds a current Centrelink Health Care Card, Pension Concession Card, or Department of Veterans' Affairs Concession Card. The Finance Committee reviews and grants discounts on school fees annually based on approved STAS status. This is a government-based financial assistance program coordinated through the school.
eligibility_criteria: Parent holds current Centrelink Health Care Card, Pension Concession Card, or Department of Veterans' Affairs Concession Card; Approved by Department for Education, Children and Young People; New enrolments or changed circumstances; Continues to previous year if already approved
special_conditions: Families approved in previous year are not required to reapply unless circumstances change. Applications are submitted to the Department for Education, Children and Young People website. School implements approved fee reductions through its Finance Committee. Subject to annual review of family circumstances.

school_name: Launceston Church Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Launceston Grammar offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and nurture students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability. These scholarships are designed for students entering primary and secondary year levels who show exceptional achievement across diverse disciplines including general reasoning, mathematics, reading comprehension and written expression.
description: Academic Scholarships at Launceston Grammar recognise students demonstrating exceptional academic ability across multiple disciplines. Applicants participate in independent Academic Testing scheduled for February each year, which spans general reasoning and problem solving, mathematics achievement and reasoning, reading comprehension, and written expression. The selection process includes the online application form, a handwritten letter to the Principal outlining reason for applying and particular strengths, and shortlisted applicants are invited for interviews. The exact financial benefit varies but scholarship applications require a non-refundable application fee of $150. Scholarships are offered on a competitive basis through the annual scholarship program which runs 12 months in advance of the intended year of entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Grade 5 through Grade 12; Applicants must be Australian citizens or Permanent Residents of Australia (evidence of residency must be provided); Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Must be available to participate in independent Academic Testing in February
special_conditions: Applications are now closed for 2026 Academic Scholarships. Successful applicants commence at Launceston Grammar in their awarded grade level the following school year. The scholarship program runs 12 months in advance of the intended year of entry.

school_name: Launceston Church Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Launceston Grammar offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding ability, leadership, and commitment to making a positive impact in their communities. These scholarships celebrate diverse talents across academic, artistic, sporting and cultural disciplines.
description: General Excellence Scholarships at Launceston Grammar recognise students demonstrating strong commitment to their studies and capability and achievement in at least two of the following fields: academic studies, performing arts, visual arts, music, sports, debating/public speaking, or community service. Applicants submit a handwritten letter to the Principal explaining why they are applying and how they demonstrate excellence in their chosen areas, along with supporting documentation uploaded to the online application form. The selection process includes shortlisted applicants being invited for interviews with the Principal. The exact financial benefit varies but is determined based on merit and need. Scholarships that recognise The Arts or Sport require ongoing study of the specific subject area and participation in the School's extra-curricular program throughout the tenure of the scholarship. The application fee is non-refundable at $110.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Grade 5 through Grade 12; Applicants must demonstrate strong commitment to studies; Applicants must demonstrate capability and achievement in at least two of the following fields: academic studies, performing arts, visual arts, music, sports, debating/public speaking, or community service; Must be available for interview if shortlisted
special_conditions: Scholarships that recognise The Arts or Sport are required to meet additional conditions, including the ongoing study of the specific subject area and participation in the School's extra-curricular program throughout the tenure of the scholarship. Successful applicants commence at Launceston Grammar in their awarded grade level the following school year.

school_name: Launceston Church Grammar School - Junior Campus
overview: Launceston Grammar offers Regional Boarding Scholarships to students whose families reside in regional areas of Tasmania. These scholarships provide financial assistance towards boarding fees for students entering Grade 5 through Grade 12 who board on a full-time basis.
description: Regional Boarding Scholarships are available to applicants entering Grades 5 to 12 and boarding on a full-time basis. The scholarship carries a rebate of up to 50% on boarding fees, as determined by the Principal. Eligible regional areas include Northern Midlands, East Coast, Northwest Coast, West Coast, Flinders Island, King Island and Southern Tasmania. Applicants must submit the online scholarship application form along with a handwritten letter to the Principal and supporting documentation. The selection process includes academic testing and interviews with the Principal. A non-refundable application fee of $110 is payable. Applicants must be available to participate in independent Academic Testing scheduled to occur on Monday 23 February 2026 at the Senior Campus, Mowbray.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Grade 5 through Grade 12; Must be boarding on a full-time basis; Family must reside in regional areas of Tasmania including Northern Midlands, East Coast, Northwest Coast, West Coast, Flinders Island, King Island and Southern Tasmania; Must be available to participate in independent Academic Testing; Must be Australian citizen or Permanent Resident of Australia
special_conditions: Scholarship determined by the Principal on a case-by-case basis. Successful applicants commence at Launceston Grammar in their awarded grade level the following school year. The scholarship program runs 12 months in advance of the intended year of entry. Full-time boarding required.

overview: Launceston Church Grammar School offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and achievement across a range of academic disciplines. The scholarship is designed for high-achieving students entering Year 7 and above. It provides access to the school's comprehensive academic program and supports talented scholars with dedicated tuition.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic performance and intellectual ability. Applicants must participate in independent Academic Testing, which is scheduled and spans various disciplines including general reasoning and problem solving, mathematics achievement and reasoning, reading comprehension, and written expression. The selection process includes academic testing, submission of a handwritten letter to the Principal outlining their reason for applying and particular strengths in a field of endeavour, and an interview with the Principal for shortlisted applicants. Applications are typically conducted 12 months in advance of intended year of entry. The scholarship provides access to the school's academic program; specific financial benefits are determined by the awarding committee and may vary. Applications close on 7 April 2026 for 2027 entry.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and above; Must demonstrate outstanding academic achievement; Australian citizenship or eligible visa residency; Must participate in academic testing on scheduled date; Handwritten letter and interview required
special_conditions: Applications are now closed for Academic Scholarships for 2027 entry. The scholarship program runs 12 months in advance of the intended year of entry.

overview: Launceston Church Grammar School offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise and nurture students who demonstrate outstanding ability, leadership, and commitment to making a positive impact in their communities. The scholarship celebrates students with diverse talents across academic, artistic, sporting and personal development areas.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional commitment to their studies and capability and achievement in at least two of the following fields: academic performance, performing arts, visual arts, sport, leadership, community service, and other areas of endeavour. Applicants must submit an online application, pay a non-refundable application fee, provide a handwritten letter to the Principal demonstrating strong commitment to studies and achievement in chosen areas, and may be invited to an interview with the Principal. The scholarship is open to students entering various year levels and commencing in the following school year. Financial benefits are determined by the awarding committee. Additional requirements apply for scholarships recognising the Arts or Sport, including ongoing study of the specific subject area and participation in the school's extra-curricular program throughout the tenure of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering multiple year levels; Must demonstrate capability and achievement in at least two fields; Must demonstrate strong commitment to studies; Scholarships recognising Arts or Sport require ongoing study of subject area and participation in extra-curricular activities throughout tenure; Australian citizenship or eligible visa residency recommended
special_conditions: Scholarships that recognise the Arts or Sport are required to meet additional conditions, including the ongoing study of the specific subject area and participation in the School's extra-curricular program throughout the tenure of the scholarship. Supporting documentation may be uploaded under 'Additional Documentation' in the online application form.

overview: Launceston Church Grammar School offers the Regional Boarding Scholarship to support students from regional, rural and remote areas of Tasmania and Australia who would otherwise not have the opportunity to attend the school. The scholarship recognises the barriers faced by geographically isolated families and provides financial assistance for both tuition and boarding fees.
description: The Regional Boarding Scholarship is designed to support students entering Grades 5 to 12 who are boarding on a full-time basis at the school. Applicants must be available to participate in independent Academic Testing, scheduled to occur on Monday 23 February 2026 at the Senior Campus in Mowbray. The Academic Testing spans various disciplines including general reasoning and problem solving, mathematics achievement and reasoning, reading comprehension, and written expression. The online scholarship application form automatically registers applicants to participate in the testing. The scholarship carries a discount on boarding fees as determined by the scholarship awarding committee. Applications open in November with assessments and interviews taking place over the following six months. The scholarship is awarded to commence the following school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Grades 5 to 12; Must be from regional, rural or remote area of Tasmania or Australia; Must be boarding on a full-time basis at the school; Must participate in academic testing; Must be Australian resident or hold appropriate visa; Handwritten letter and interview required
special_conditions: The scholarship carries a discount on boarding fees as determined by the scholarship awarding committee. Students must board on a full-time basis. The scholarship is subject to the same renewal conditions and requirements as all school scholarships, including good conduct and satisfactory academic progress.

overview: Launceston Church Grammar School offers Boarding Scholarships to students entering Grades 5 to 12 who will be boarding on a full-time basis. The scholarship recognises the need to support boarding students and provides financial assistance through a discount on boarding fees.
description: The Boarding Scholarship is available to applicants entering Grades 5 to 12 and boarding on a full-time basis at the school. The scholarship carries a discount on boarding fees as determined by the scholarship awarding committee. Specific financial benefits vary and are assessed on an individual basis. The scholarship application process requires submission of an online application form with payment of the non-refundable application fee, a handwritten letter to the Principal, and participation in an interview. The scholarship tenure is subject to annual review and renewal conditions must be maintained throughout the student's time at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Grades 5 to 12; Must be boarding on a full-time basis; Must demonstrate commitment to school values and conduct; Subject to annual review; Satisfactory academic progress required
special_conditions: The scholarship carries a discount on boarding fees only as determined by the scholarship awarding committee. Recipients must maintain good conduct and satisfactory academic progress. The scholarship is subject to annual review and may be adjusted or withdrawn if conditions are not met.

overview: Learning for Life is a partnership program with The Smith Family that provides financial support to help families with education-related costs. The program assists students with purchasing books, uniforms, and paying for school excursions.
description: Learning for Life is a family partnership program delivered through The Smith Family in partnership with Lilydale District School. The program provides financial support to help with costs related to education such as books, uniforms and excursions. Students and families must commit to regular school attendance, providing yearly progress reports, and maintaining contact with The Smith Family. The program is non-monetary in nature but provides support through coordinated assistance with education-related expenses. Families interested in applying should contact the school directly and complete the referral form provided by the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families of enrolled students; Students must commit to regular school attendance; Parents/guardians must return yearly progress reports; Must maintain contact with The Smith Family coordinator; Assessed on individual family circumstances
special_conditions: Program operates as a partnership between families and The Smith Family. Recipients must maintain regular attendance and provide yearly progress reports to remain in the program. Participation is voluntary and referral-based through school channels.

overview: Leighland Christian School offers Fee Assistance to low-income families to ensure Christian education is accessible to all. The school recognises that financial circumstances can present barriers to education and works with families to provide affordable fee options.
description: The Fee Assistance Program is designed for families experiencing financial hardship. Low-income families are encouraged to apply for Fee Assistance, and arrangements can be made with the Business Manager, Mr David Grey. The program also extends to parents in recognised full-time Christian Ministry roles or those on leave from the mission field, who can arrange for Fee Assistance under a discount fee structure. The exact financial benefit and application process are determined on a case-by-case basis through discussion with school leadership. Renewal or continued eligibility may be subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Low-income families; Parents in recognised full-time Christian Ministry role; Parents on leave from mission field; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Applications and arrangements are made directly with the Business Manager. Fee Assistance eligibility may be subject to annual review based on family financial circumstances.

overview: Leighland Christian School provides a fee discount incentive for families who pay their full annual fees in advance before Term 1 commences. This program rewards early payment with a 5% discount on the total annual fee.
description: The Early Payment Fee Discount provides families with a 5% reduction on their annual school fees if the entire year's fees are paid before Term 1 commences. This financial assistance program is available to all families and provides a meaningful saving for those who are able to manage early payment. The discount applies to the total annual tuition fees and helps to make school fees more affordable across the year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Must pay full annual fees before Term 1 commences; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Discount only applies if the entire year's fees are paid before Term 1 commences.

overview: Leighland Christian School provides progressive sibling discounts to make education more affordable for larger families. The discount structure rewards families enrolling multiple children.
description: The Sibling Fee Discount program offers a tiered discount structure for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The second child receives 50% discount on fees, the third child receives 65% discount, and the fourth child onwards receive 100% fee remission (no charge). These discounts are applied automatically to families with siblings and help significantly reduce the financial burden for larger families, making education more accessible across all socioeconomic backgrounds.
eligibility_criteria: Must have at least one sibling enrolled at the school; Open to all families; Available for all year levels
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction, 65% Fee Reduction, or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Discounts apply as follows: Second child 50%, Third child 65%, Fourth child and onwards 100% (no charge). These discounts are in addition to other fee payment options available.

overview: Marist Regional College offers Senior College Scholarships to current Year 10 students and prospective students entering Year 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate excellence in academic, co-curricular and outreach activities while living out the College mission and values.
description: The Senior College Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 11 and Year 12 at Marist Regional College. The scholarship provides up to 50% discount on the student's net tuition fees for both Year 11 and Year 12 education at the College. The selection process involves submission of a written letter (no longer than two pages) demonstrating how the applicant meets the scholarship criteria, followed by an interview with the Principal. Scholarships are awarded at the discretion of the Principal, following consultation with staff and a Scholarship Selection Committee comprising seven members: Principal, College Board Chair, Year Ten Coordinator(s), Deputy Principal(s), and Director of Faith and Mission. The scholarship will continue during the recipient's schooling at Marist Regional College unless their enrolment is withdrawn. Students currently receiving Fee Assistance are also eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 10 students at Marist Regional College or prospective students entering Year 11; Students must fulfill most, but not necessarily all criteria; Evidence of contributions in academic, co-curricular and outreach opportunities; Demonstrates alignment with College mission and values; Overall capacity to enhance the life and culture of the College; Students currently receiving Fee Assistance are eligible
special_conditions: The scholarship recognises Year 10 students who live out the College mission and values with respect to academic, co-curricular and outreach opportunities. Recipients of the scholarship are expected to positively apply themselves to all aspects of their education and College life. In instances where a scholarship recipient does not make satisfactory progress, fails to embrace the College mission and values or does not cooperate in all aspects of their education, the Principal may temporarily or permanently withdraw the scholarship. Scholarships can be reviewed at the Principal's discretion. In instances where a recipient leaves Marist Regional College to transfer to another local College to continue their education, families may be required to pay back the scholarship funds that have been applied to their tuition expense. In cases where families are required to relocate and therefore enrolment is withdrawn, the scholarship will simply discontinue.

overview: Marist Regional College awards Marian Community Scholarships to one student per year level from Year 7 to Year 11. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate commitment to the College's Catholic mission and community values.
description: The Marian Community Scholarship is an award program offered to outstanding students at Marist Regional College across multiple year levels. Recipients are selected on an annual basis, with one student selected from each of Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, and Year 11. The scholarships recognise students who embody the Catholic ethos and values of the College and make meaningful contributions to the school community. While the exact financial value and specific selection criteria are not fully detailed in the official documentation accessed, the program is presented as a prestigious honour reflecting academic and community excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7; Open to students in Year 8; Open to students in Year 9; Open to students in Year 10; Open to students in Year 11; One student selected per year level; Demonstrates alignment with College mission and Catholic values; Evidence of community contribution

overview: Marist Regional College provides Fee Assistance to families experiencing financial hardship. This support program ensures no student is disadvantaged by financial circumstances and enables access to quality Catholic education for all families.
description: Fee Assistance is available to families experiencing financial difficulty or hardship. The College has committed to ensuring no child is held back by fees or uniform expenses, guaranteeing equal access to all that the College offers. Families struggling with fees can contact the Principal or Business Manager directly to discuss support options. The College recognises that families can experience unexpected financial setbacks due to serious illness, loss of job or other calamitous events, and alternative payment arrangements may be considered or negotiated on a case-by-case basis. This is part of the College's Catholic education philosophy that no child will be denied a place in a Catholic school because of financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; Families experiencing financial hardship; Families unable to afford full fees or uniform expenses; No specific income threshold stated; Assessed on case-by-case basis
special_conditions: Confidential process. Families should contact the Principal or Business Manager (Mr Andrew Leary: aleary@mrc.tas.edu.au) directly to discuss their circumstances. All financial information is kept strictly confidential. Support may include flexible payment plans or adjusted fee arrangements. The College Principal and current staff have personally received fee assistance when students and staff members were in financial need.

overview: Montello Primary School provides access to the Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) for families experiencing financial difficulty. This state-funded program supports eligible families by eliminating school levies, ensuring all students can participate in learning activities without financial barriers.
description: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) is a Tasmanian Government program that provides financial assistance to families who hold a Health Care Card, Low Income Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card. Once approved for STAS, families do not need to pay any school levies. The program is delivered through the Department for Education, Children and Young People. There is no application fee. Families can apply online or collect a paper application form from the school office. The school also provides access to the Spectacles Assistance Scheme to support children who require spectacles. Additionally, families experiencing financial hardship can contact the School Business Manager Emma Short on 6432 2755 or emma.short@decyp.tas.gov.au, or the Department of Education's Financial Services Unit on 1800 827 055 or Fin.Serv@decyp.tas.gov.au for confidential discussions about alternative support options including payment plans.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a Health Care Card, Low Income Health Care Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Parents/guardians must be residents of Tasmania; Humanitarian, refugee, and asylum seeker visa holders are eligible
special_conditions: Students will not be prevented from participating in any learning activities that are part of the curriculum if their levies have not been paid. Payment plans are available for all families and may continue through to the end of the school year. Matters are dealt with directly with parents and not through the child.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in entering students. The scholarship is designed to support girls who demonstrate exceptional intellectual ability and commitment to learning excellence.
description: The Academic Scholarship at Mount Carmel College rewards students who have demonstrated outstanding academic performance and intellectual achievement. Selection is based on the applicant's academic track record and potential for continued excellence. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction to successful recipients. Students submit a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking their response to selection criteria. Shortlisted candidates are invited to attend an interview with key College staff. The scholarship is available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Current and prospective students eligible; Demonstrated outstanding academic performance; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No scholarship examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff. Each scholarship has different criteria and performance measures.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers the Arts Scholarship to support girls with exceptional talent and motivation in the performing and visual arts. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate dedication to dance, drama, music, or visual art and seek to extend themselves in their chosen artistic discipline.
description: The Arts Scholarship recognises excellence and dedication in dance, drama, music, and visual art. The scholarship is available to students with demonstrated motivation to participate in selected arts disciplines and a willingness to extend themselves and develop their artistic talents further. Successful recipients receive tuition fee reduction as part of their scholarship package. The scholarship selection process involves submission of a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking response to the specific arts discipline selection criteria. Shortlisted applicants are invited to an interview with College staff members. Available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Current and prospective students eligible; Demonstrated talent and motivation in Dance, Drama, Music, or Visual Art; Willingness to extend artistic abilities; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No formal examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria for chosen art form. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff to discuss artistic talents and commitment.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers Sport Scholarships to recognise girls who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to athletic excellence. The scholarship supports students who show promise and dedication across a range of sports offered at the College.
description: The Sport Scholarship recognises girls who demonstrate outstanding athletic ability and commitment to sporting excellence. The scholarship is designed for students who show exceptional promise in any sport offered at Mount Carmel College, including netball, basketball, hockey, swimming, cricket, athletics, cross country, and soccer. Successful recipients receive tuition fee reduction to support their continued participation and development. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting talent and commitment. Applicants submit a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking response to sport-specific selection criteria. Shortlisted candidates are invited to interview with College staff. Available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Current and prospective students eligible; Demonstrated exceptional sporting ability and commitment; Participation in or potential for school sports programs; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No formal examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff to discuss sporting achievements and future potential. College participates in SATIS (Sports Association of Tasmanian Independent Schools) competitions.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers the Sisters of Charity Scholarship to honour the College's founding legacy. The scholarship recognises girls who follow in the footsteps of the Sisters of Charity through service to their community and demonstrate a commitment to social justice and environmental stewardship.
description: The Sisters of Charity Scholarship recognises students who embody the values and vision of the College's founders, the Sisters of Charity. The scholarship is awarded to girls who demonstrate service to their community and commitment to social justice and environmental sustainability. Successful recipients receive tuition fee reduction to support their continued studies. Selection is based on demonstrated community service, social justice awareness, and environmental commitment. Applicants submit a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking response to the selection criteria around community service and values alignment with the Sisters of Charity legacy. Shortlisted candidates are invited to interview with College staff. Available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Current and prospective students eligible; Demonstrated service to community; Commitment to social justice; Environmental awareness and action; Alignment with Sisters of Charity values; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No formal examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria focused on community service, social justice, and environmental commitment. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff to discuss service work and values alignment.

overview: Mount Carmel College offers the All Rounder Scholarship to recognise girls who demonstrate high achievement in academic studies combined with active participation in co-curricular activities. The scholarship supports students who exhibit excellence and balance across multiple areas of school life.
description: The All Rounder Scholarship recognises girls who achieve high academic performance while actively participating in the full range of co-curricular endeavours. The scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate excellence across multiple domains including academics, sports, arts, leadership, and service. Successful recipients receive tuition fee reduction. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement combined with significant involvement and contribution to co-curricular programs. Applicants submit a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking response to selection criteria emphasising balanced excellence. Shortlisted candidates are invited to interview with College staff to discuss their achievements and commitments. Available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Current and prospective students eligible; High academic achievement; Active participation in co-curricular activities; Demonstrated excellence in multiple areas; Leadership and service involvement; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No formal examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria demonstrating balanced achievement across academics and co-curricular areas. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship
overview: Mount Carmel College offers the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship to support girls who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. The scholarship recognises the unique perspectives and contributions of First Nations students and provides access to quality education.
description: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship is awarded to girls who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander and are accepted as such by the community in which they live. The scholarship is designed to provide educational access and support to First Nations students. Successful recipients receive tuition fee reduction. Selection is based on demonstrated Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander identity and community acceptance. Applicants submit a one-page handwritten summary outlining reasons for application and linking response to selection criteria around cultural identity and community connection. Shortlisted candidates are invited to interview with College staff. Available for current and prospective students entering Years 5 and 7 in 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5 or Year 7; Identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander; Accepted as such by the community in which student lives; Current and prospective students eligible; Must be eligible for enrolment at Mount Carmel College
special_conditions: No formal examinations required. Applicants submit one-page handwritten summary linking response to selection criteria. Must identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander and be accepted as such by their community. Shortlisted candidates attend interview with College staff.

school_name: North West Support School - Miandetta Campus
Our Lady of Mercy Catholic School

overview: North West Christian School offers a Fee Assistance Program to support families experiencing financial hardship. The program provides partial fee reduction based on combined gross family income. Families with demonstrated financial need may be eligible for up to 25% reduction on tuition fees.
description: North West Christian School provides fee assistance to eligible families to make Christian education more accessible. To be eligible for fee assistance, a family's combined gross family income must be below $51,800. If eligible for fee assistance, an amount of up to 25% off the tuition fee will be applied, less any applicable family discount. The program is designed to support families in financial need and requires families to contact the school to discuss their specific circumstances and negotiate payment options. Families experiencing difficulties with fee payments are encouraged to contact the school to discuss alternative payment plans before fees become overdue. The school emphasizes early contact to help develop sustainable payment arrangements.
eligibility_criteria: Combined gross family income below $51,800; Current or prospective student enrolled at the school; Contact school to apply and discuss circumstances
special_conditions: Fee assistance is applied to tuition fees only. Sibling discounts apply separately. Families must maintain current payment status and negotiate arrangements with the school directly. Applicants must contact the school first before fees become overdue to establish alternative payment plans.

school_name: Our Lady of Lourdes Catholic School

overview: Prospect High School participates in the STAS program, which provides financial assistance to low-income families towards the cost of school levies. This scheme is administered by the Department for Education, Children and Young People and supports students from kindergarten through to Year 12.
description: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) is financial assistance available to assist with the cost of school levies. The scheme provides assistance for low-income families towards the cost of levies for students enrolled from kindergarten through to grade 12. Funding for eligible applicants will be provided directly to the school. Families approved for STAS are not required to pay the Inclusive Levy, however, may still be required to pay "Other Charges" for optional activities not included in the standard levy. Payment arrangements and instalments can be negotiated by contacting the School Office.
eligibility_criteria: Parents/guardians must be residents of Tasmania; Low-income family status; Humanitarian, refugee, and asylum seeker visa holders eligible; International students must seek advice from Government Education and Training International
special_conditions: Application must be submitted each school year. Confidential discussion available directly with school. Families approved for STAS may still be required to pay Other Charges for selected additional activities.

school_name: St Aloysius Catholic College - Kingston Beach Campus
St Finn Barr's Catholic Primary School

overview: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) provides assistance to low income families towards the cost of levies for students enrolled in St Marys District School from kindergarten through to year 12. STAS is provided through school and college resourcing rather than payment direct to families.
description: The Student Assistance Scheme (STAS) provides assistance to low income families towards the cost of levies for students enrolled in St Marys District School from kindergarten through to year 12. Families on low incomes who meet the eligibility conditions of STAS will not be required to pay school levies. Grandparents raising grandchildren may also be eligible for STAS. The scheme is provided through school and college resourcing rather than payment direct to families. Families who are not eligible for STAS but who may experience financial hardship should talk to the school about payment arrangements and other financial assistance which may help.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families with low income; Eligible students from Kindergarten to Grade 12; Grandparents raising grandchildren may be eligible; Must meet published income eligibility conditions
special_conditions: STAS is provided through school and college resourcing rather than payment direct to families. School staff understand that financial matters are highly sensitive and will remain completely confidential. These matters will be dealt with directly with families and not through the child. The child's participation in learning will not be affected.

overview: St Mary's College supports Catholic families experiencing financial hardship to ensure children are not excluded from a Catholic school education due to inability to pay fees. The College works with families to provide financial support on a case-by-case basis.
description: As part of St Mary's College's fee policy, the school fully supports the Catholic principle that Catholic children should not be excluded from Catholic school education if their parents or carers are unable to pay school fees. Families experiencing financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the school directly to discuss their circumstances. The College provides individualised fee assistance based on financial need. No specific financial value is published as assistance is tailored to individual family circumstances. Families must demonstrate financial need through confidential discussions with the enrolment officer. The assistance is provided for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Catholic families experiencing financial hardship; Inability to afford school fees; Must be enrolled or seeking to enrol at St Mary's College
special_conditions: Families experiencing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the school directly. Fee assistance is provided on a case-by-case basis and discussions are confidential. The school's commitment is to ensure Catholic children are not excluded due to inability to afford fees.

overview: St Patrick's College offers the Thomas Bourke Memorial Scholarship to support Year 7 students who are admitted to the college. Established from the estate of Thomas Bourke, a significant early supporter of the college, the scholarship provides financial assistance through a deduction of annual school fees.
description: Thomas Bourke was a significant contributor to the welfare of St Patrick's College in its early years, with part of his estate left for the purpose of supporting students at the College. The scholarship is allocated at the discretion of the Principal and is awarded to Year 7 students. Recipients of the Thomas Bourke Memorial Scholarship receive a deduction of their annual school fees each year for the duration of their enrolment at the College. The allocation and continuation of the scholarship is based on satisfactory academic progress and conduct. If a student fails to make satisfactory progress or exhibits unsatisfactory conduct, the scholarship may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; At the discretion of Principal; Satisfactory academic progress required; Good conduct required; Enrolled at St Patrick's College
special_conditions: Scholarships may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if student fails to make satisfactory progress or exhibits unsatisfactory conduct. Recipients must maintain good standing throughout their enrolment.

overview: St Patrick's College offers the Martin-Lyall Scholarship established through the generosity of the late Mrs Rosemary Gleeson-West. The scholarship assists students with payment of school fees and is available to Year 10 and Year 11 students.
description: The Martin-Lyall Scholarship was established through the generous donation of the late Mrs Rosemary Gleeson-West, who passed away in 2016. The scholarship was created in memory of her parents, Mr Athol Winton Martin and Maida Kathleen Lyall, who had connections to Tasmania and St Patrick's College. Annually, during Term 3, students in Year 10 and Year 11 are invited to apply by submitting an essay comprising two sections in accordance with the scholarship guidelines. After consideration of written applications, a shortlist of students is selected for an interview. Two students from both year groups are then awarded the scholarship, which is a contribution deducted from their annual fee at St Patrick's College for one school year.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 or Year 11 students; Application during Term 3; Must submit essay in two sections; Shortlisted applicants attend interview; One school year award
number_awarded: 2 per year from each year level (4 total annually)
special_conditions: Applications are essay-based. Shortlisted candidates progress to interview stage. Award is for one school year only. Scholarship may be withdrawn if recipient fails to make satisfactory progress or exhibits unsatisfactory conduct.

overview: St Patrick's College awards Principal's Bursaries to Year 10 students each year in Term 3. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal following consultation with staff and recognise students who live out the College mission and values.
description: Each year the Principal awards 20 bursaries to Year 10 students in Term 3. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal following consultation with staff, and recognise and reward students who live out the College mission and values. The bursary provides a 50% discount of the student's net tuition fees in their Year 11 and Year 12. The bursary will be awarded based on demonstrated commitment to College values and may be reviewed annually. If a student fails to make satisfactory progress in their studies or exhibits unsatisfactory conduct in any other way, the bursary may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; At the discretion of Principal following staff consultation; Must demonstrate commitment to College mission and values; Satisfactory academic progress required; Good conduct required
special_conditions: Awarded at discretion of Principal following staff consultation. Applies to Year 11 and Year 12 tuition fees. May be subject to annual review. May be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if student fails to maintain satisfactory progress or conduct.

overview: The Francine Allan Award is presented annually to a Year 12 female student who demonstrates exceptional all-round ability, enthusiasm, and leadership. It recognises a student who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment.
description: The Francine Allan Award is in memory of the late Francine Gayle Allan, who gained her Higher School Certificate at St Patrick's College after amalgamation with Marian College, which she attended for her secondary education. Francine was nearing completion of her second year of studies at university when she tragically died in a motor vehicle accident. Each year, Year 12 female students vote for a female Year 12 student they feel is an all-rounder who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment. This is an extremely prestigious award voted for by the Year 12 cohort to recognise exceptional all-around contribution and leadership qualities. The award is presented at the Year 12 Valete Mass and Celebration Evening.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 female students; Nominated and voted by Year 12 students; Demonstrates all-round ability; Shows exceptional contribution to school community
special_conditions: This is a non-monetary award. The award is determined by peer vote from Year 12 students. Presented at the Valete Mass and Celebration Evening. Recognises exceptional all-round ability and contribution to the school community.

overview: The Garry Wainwright Award is presented annually to a Year 12 male student who demonstrates exceptional all-round ability, enthusiasm, and leadership. It recognises a student who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment.
description: The Garry Wainwright Award commemorates Garry, who began his education at St Patrick's College as a Grade 3 student in 1966 and was well-liked and respected throughout his school years. Garry was a talented swimmer and well known in the sporting field throughout Tasmania. He was a member and trainer of College football teams and a member of the 1st VIII Rowing Crew. On 19 January 1976, Garry died in a car accident. Each year, the Year 12 cohort votes for a male Year 12 student they feel is an all-rounder who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment. This is an extremely prestigious award voted for by the Year 12 cohort to recognise exceptional all-around contribution and leadership qualities. The award is presented at the Year 12 Valete Mass and Celebration Evening.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 male students; Nominated and voted by Year 12 students; Demonstrates all-round ability; Shows exceptional contribution to school community
special_conditions: This is a non-monetary award. The award is determined by peer vote from Year 12 students. Presented at the Valete Mass and Celebration Evening. Recognises exceptional all-round ability and contribution to the school community.

overview: The Michael Zeeman Award is presented annually to a Year 10 student of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life. It recognises a student who demonstrates effort, participation, and positive values in their contribution to the school community.
description: The Michael Zeeman Award commemorates Old Collegian Michael Zeeman, who tragically died during a medical procedure a short time after completing Year 10. Michael loved life, valued fairness, and appreciated that effort and taking part were more important than being first. Michael was an all-rounder, had a great sense of humour, and enjoyed the company of others. The Michael Zeeman Award recognises a Year 10 student of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life, reflecting the values that Michael embodied. The award reflects a belief that effort and participation are more important than being first, and honours students who contribute positively to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Demonstrates exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life; Shows effort and participation; Exhibits positive values
special_conditions: This is a non-monetary award. Award is presented in recognition of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life. Emphasises effort and participation over academic or sporting achievement alone.

overview: The Ketchell Participation Award is an annual award for a Year 7 student who has, in simple and joyful ways, contributed positively to the College through generous and genuine participation.
description: The Ketchell Participation Award honours a Year 7 student who has, in simple and joyful ways, contributed positively to the College through generous and genuine participation. The award was established in memory of Ketchell, a Year 7 student in 2015 who had only been a member of St Patrick's College for eight months when she tragically lost her battle with a viral infection. In the eight months at the College, Ketchell brought her tremendous gifts of exuberance, compassion, and positivity. The award recognises the spirit of participation and positive contribution that Ketchell embodied, celebrating Year 7 students who demonstrate similar qualities of genuine engagement with college life.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Contributes positively through genuine participation; Demonstrates exuberance and compassion; Shows positive engagement with College community
special_conditions: This is a non-monetary award. Award recognises genuine and joyful participation in College life. Celebrates qualities of exuberance, compassion, and positivity.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: St Patrick's College offers the Thomas Bourke Memorial Scholarship to Year 7 students to assist with payment of school fees. The scholarship recognises the generosity of Thomas Bourke, a significant contributor to the College in its early years, whose estate was left to support students at the College.
description: The Thomas Bourke Memorial Scholarship is awarded at the discretion of the Principal to Year 7 students. Recipients receive an annual deduction of their school fees. The scholarship provides assistance with tuition costs and is available through the generosity of Thomas Bourke's estate. The scholarship may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if a student fails to make satisfactory progress or is unsatisfactory in any other way. This scholarship is awarded to students once they are attending the College and supports them throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Allocation at the discretion of the Principal; Satisfactory academic progress required; No disciplinary issues
special_conditions: Allocation at the discretion of the Principal. Scholarship may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if student fails to make satisfactory progress or is unsatisfactory in any other way. Award made to students once they are attending the College.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: St Patrick's College offers the Martin-Lyall Scholarship to assist students with payment of school fees, in memory of Mr Athol Winton Martin and Maida Kathleen Lyall. The scholarship was established through the generous donation of the late Mrs Rosemary Gleeson-West.
description: The Martin-Lyall Scholarship is available to Year 10 and 11 students. Annually during Term 3, eligible students are invited to apply by submitting a two-section essay in accordance with scholarship guidelines. After written application review, shortlisted students are selected for an interview. Two students from each year group are awarded the scholarship, which provides a contribution deducted from their annual fee at St Patrick's College for one school year. The selection process recognises students who demonstrate commitment to their studies and the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 and Year 11 students; Application during Term 3; Must submit two-section essay; Shortlist required for interview; Enrolment at St Patrick's College
special_conditions: Applications invited annually during Term 3. Essay must comprise two sections in accordance with scholarship guidelines. Shortlisted students selected for interview. Award covers one school year only. Both year groups (Year 10 and 11) eligible with two awards per group.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: St Patrick's College awards 20 bursaries annually to Year 10 students to recognise and reward those who exemplify the College mission and values. These bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal following consultation with staff.
description: Each year the Principal awards 20 bursaries to Year 10 students. These bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal following consultation with staff, and recognise and reward students who live out the College mission and values. The bursary is valued at 50% discount of the student's net tuition fees in their Year 11 and Year 12. Recipients must maintain satisfactory progress and conduct. The bursary may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if a student fails to make satisfactory progress or is unsatisfactory in any other way. This is a substantial financial assistance program benefiting 20 students annually.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Demonstrates alignment with College mission and values; Satisfactory academic progress; Good conduct; Enrolment at St Patrick's College
special_conditions: Awards made by Principal discretion following consultation with staff. 20 bursaries awarded annually to Year 10 students. Value is 50% discount of net tuition fees in Years 11 and 12. Bursary may be temporarily or permanently withdrawn if student fails to make satisfactory progress or is unsatisfactory in any other way. Recipients already attending the College.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: The Francine Allan Award recognises an outstanding female Year 12 student who demonstrates exceptional all-round ability, enthusiasm, and leadership. The award is in memory of Francine Gayle Allan, a St Patrick's College alumna.
description: The Francine Allan Award is presented annually in memory of Francine Gayle Allan, who gained her Higher School Certificate at St Patrick's College after completing her secondary education at Marian College. Francine was recognised for her all-round ability, enthusiasm and leadership, and was invested as a prefect in her final year before tragically dying in a motor vehicle accident while completing her studies at the University of Tasmania. Each year, Year 12 female students vote for a female Year 12 student they feel is an all-rounder who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment. The award celebrates students who embody Francine's spirit of service and dedication.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 female students; Demonstrated all-round ability in academics and co-curricular activities; Significant contribution to school community; Humility and commitment to service
special_conditions: Annual award. Recipient determined by Year 12 female student vote. Award recognises all-round contribution to school community with humility and commitment. Presented at end of year.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: The Garry Wainwright Award recognises an outstanding male Year 12 student who demonstrates exceptional all-round contribution to the school community. The award is in memory of Garry Wainwright, a respected former student and talented sportsman.
description: The Garry Wainwright Award is presented annually in memory of Garry Wainwright, who began his education at St Patrick's College as a Grade 3 student in 1966. Garry was well liked and respected throughout his school years and was a talented swimmer and well known in the sporting field throughout Tasmania. He was a member and trainer of College football teams and a member of the 1st VIII Rowing Crew, tragically dying in a car accident on 19 January 1976. Each year, the Year 12 cohort vote for a male Year 12 student they feel is an all-rounder who contributes to others and the school community with humility and commitment. The award honours students who embody Garry's dedication to sport and community service.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 male students; Demonstrated all-round ability across sporting and academic activities; Significant contribution to school community; Humility and commitment to service
special_conditions: Annual award. Recipient determined by Year 12 male student vote. Award recognises all-round contribution to school community with humility and commitment. Presented at end of year.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: The Michael Zeeman Award recognises a Year 10 student of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life. The award is in memory of Michael Zeeman, an Old Collegian who exemplified dedication to participation and effort.
description: The Michael Zeeman Award commemorates Old Collegian Michael Zeeman, who tragically died during a medical procedure a short time after completing Year 10. Michael loved life, valued fairness, and appreciated that effort and taking part were more important than being first. Michael was an all-rounder with a great sense of humour who enjoyed the company of others. The award recognises a Year 10 student of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life, honouring those who demonstrate the same spirit of enthusiasm, fairness, and participation that defined Michael's character. The award celebrates students who contribute positively to College life across multiple domains.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life; Demonstrated participation in academic, sporting, and co-curricular activities; Positive contribution to school community
special_conditions: Annual award. Recognises Year 10 student of exceptional commitment to all aspects of College life. Award celebrates effort, participation and contribution across multiple domains. Presented at end of year.

school_name: St Patrick’s College - Croagh Patrick Centre
overview: The Ketchell Participation Award recognises a Year 7 student who has contributed positively to the College through generous and genuine participation. The award is in memory of Tahlia Ketchell, a Year 7 student at St Patrick's College.
description: The Ketchell Participation Award is an award for a Year 7 student who has, in simple and joyful ways, contributed positively to the College through generous and genuine participation. Tahlia Ketchell was a Year 7 student in 2015 and had only been a member of St Patrick's College for eight months when she tragically lost her battle with a viral infection. In the eight months at the College, Tahlia brought her tremendous gifts of exuberance, compassion and positivity. The award honours her legacy by recognising Year 7 students who demonstrate similar joy, generosity, and positive engagement with the College community. This award celebrates students who make meaningful contributions through participation and service.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students; Demonstrated positive contribution to College; Generous and genuine participation; Exuberance and compassion in school community engagement
special_conditions: Annual award. Recognises Year 7 student contributing positively through generous and genuine participation. Award celebrates exuberance, compassion and positivity in service to the College community. Presented at end of year.

program_name: Financial Assistance for Families in Hardship
overview: St Peter Chanel Catholic School recognises that some families may face financial barriers to Catholic education. The school provides discretionary financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship who are committed to supporting the Catholic ethos.
description: St Peter Chanel Catholic School is committed to ensuring that no child is denied a place in the school because of financial hardship. Families who are interested in Catholic education and feel they could benefit from financial support are encouraged to contact the School directly. The school operates as part of the Catholic Education Tasmania system, which is administered by the Tasmanian Catholic Education Office.
